description: Large natural park on Puget Sound. Excellent foraging conditions, clean soil, no pesticide spraying.
Historic park in Capitol Hill. Recently sprayed with Glyphosate to control weeds. Do not forage here.
Highly visited urban lake park. Sprayed 20 days ago, past the 14-day safety window. Generally safe, but exercise caution.
Industrial history park. Soil contains dangerous levels of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs), lead, and arsenic. Completely unsafe for foraging.
Central Capitol Hill park. Safe soil levels, no recent spraying. Good for picking dandelions in designated grassy lawns.
Old-growth forest park on Bailey Peninsula. Exceptionally clean, pristine soil, and pesticide-free. Ideal foraging area.
Waterfront park north of downtown. Sprayed yesterday with 2,4-D. Highly toxic environment, foraging forbidden.
Sandy beach park in Ballard. Clean soil, no spraying, safe for beach pea and other shoreline plants.
Art park on transformed industrial site. Safe but slightly higher background lead levels. Keep foraging light.
Beacon Hill park. Sprayed 5 days ago with Glyphosate. Not safe to forage.
